items.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
items.summary: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

items.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
items.summary: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

items.title: Uber has always wanted to be more than a ride; now it has reason to hurry
items.summary: The company has been trying to embed itself inside the AV industry — as a data provider, an investor, and a distribution platform — but the consumer-facing bet may be just as important.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Lime’s IPO gamble
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

items.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
items.summary: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

items.title: The hottest place for startups to strike a deal? The F1 paddock
items.summary: F1 Grands Prix have emerged as a new place to see and be seen if you are a founder or investor.

items.title: Voice AI in India is hard. Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway.
items.summary: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

items.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
items.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

items.title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
items.summary: Parker, a well-funded startup offering corporate credit cards and banking services, has filed for bankruptcy and is widely reported to have shut down.

items.title: GM agrees to pay $12.75M in California driver privacy settlement
items.summary: General Motors has reached a privacy-related settlement with a group of law enforcement agencies led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta.

items.title: The Instax Wide 400 builds on instant photography’s simplicity and stretches it, literally
items.summary: In an AI and digital world, analog instant film and retro-style cameras continue to remain popular, fueled by a mix of both nostalgia and novelty.

items.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
items.summary: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

items.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
items.summary: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

items.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind
items.summary: The invisible force behind all of this is no mystery to anyone paying attention to the city's tech economy. San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

items.title: Prime Video follows Netflix and Disney by adding a TikTok-like ‘Clips’ feed in its app
items.summary: The Clips feed aims to enable discovery by offering users a scrollable feed with short snippets of shows and movies.

items.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
items.summary: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

items.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
items.summary: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

items.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
items.summary: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to make painful cuts — including our subsidiaries."

items.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
items.summary: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

items.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
items.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.
